finding: Embedded-PG lifecycle moved from ziee core/database/mod.rs. Verified line-by-line vs the original: Settings population, 5-attempt init retry, 10-attempt connect backoff (100ms<<min(6)), the DATABASE_POOL/POSTGRESQL_INSTANCE OnceCells, the post-init SELECT 1 re-check, and every println!/eprintln! string are byte-verbatim. Only the 3 declared seams (migrator, pg_ctl version, pgvector hooks) + config-type differ (TRANSFORMS D1/D2).

The embedded PG URL is logged redacted (scheme://host:port/path only, never the auto-generated password) — 14-core F-12. Preserved verbatim in the move (the url::Url parse + component print). No credential-leak regression.

The panic-hook + Drop cleanup (Handle::try_current → block_in_place vs Runtime::new double-fault fix, 14-core F-09), CLEANUP_REGISTERED swap-once, and the _CLEANUP LazyLock are process-global and moved verbatim. There is exactly one ziee-framework in the dep graph → one instance of each static, same as one ziee process before. cleanup_database reads both OnceCells from the same crate now (no split ownership).

grep confirms ZERO compile-time query!/query_as!/query_scalar! in embedded_pg.rs (only runtime sqlx::query("SELECT 1") + migrator.run). ziee-framework stays build-DB-free — cd sdk && cargo check --workspace needs no DATABASE_URL (exit 0 confirmed).

pgvector interleave ordering: original was setup()→install→start()→smoke→url→store→register_cleanup. Moved: setup()→after_setup()→start()→smoke_test().await→url→store→register_cleanup. Byte-identical ordering; the hooks reproduce install_into/has_real_artifacts + CREATE EXTENSION + mark_available with the same log strings (ziee side).

Shim keeps initialize_database(&Config)->Arc<PgPool>, get_database_pool()->Result<_,sqlx::Error>, cleanup_database() byte-signature-identical. Every consumer (main.rs:206/428, lib.rs:351/431/670, file/geometry_backfill.rs:9) compiles unchanged (cargo check -p ziee exit 0).

MERGED_MIGRATOR carries set_ignore_missing(true) exactly as the original inline .set_ignore_missing(true). This is NOT an apply-untrusted-migrations relaxation (14-core F-21) — it only tolerates already-applied desktop rows the server binary doesn't own. Semantics preserved.

ZieeServerBoot::boot()'s route-builder closure is the exact extraction of the old start_backend_server closure (init_desktop_repositories, merge desktop_routes + Extension(jwt) + create_cors_layer, cfg(debug) Vite proxy / cfg(not) static-file+compression fallback). Same layers, same order. jwt captured via a boot-local OnceLock instead of the module static (Decision 4).

BootHandle{addr,pool,jwt} is app-internal (never serialized to the wire — E8 golden byte-identical confirms no schema/DTO moved). shutdown() = ziee::cleanup_server() (unchanged). The desktop CORS re-layer + Extension(jwt) on merged routes are preserved verbatim — no auth/CORS regression on the embedded server.

JWT_SERVICE + the new SERVER_POOL are set from handle.{jwt,pool} right after boot() returns (before migrations), vs the old JWT_SERVICE.set inside the setup-time closure. Later by a few statements but still well before create_main_window; auto_login is frontend-invoked only after the window loads. No observable ordering change. Post-boot step order (migrations→host_mount→ensure_admin→backfill→memory→set_ready→window) is byte-identical.

mint_admin_login now takes (pool,jwt) and uses UserRepository::new(pool) instead of Repos.user; pool instead of Repos.pool(). UserRepository is the same ziee_auth::user::UserRepository type Repos.user is, built from the same pool → get_by_username + mint_session_tokens byte-behaviourally identical. Error strings ("Failed to get admin", "Admin not found - server may still be starting", "Failed to generate tokens") preserved verbatim.

auto_login sources pool from the SERVER_POOL stash (set from BootHandle) + jwt from JWT_SERVICE; returns the same jti-whitelisted revocable session via the shared mint_session_tokens path. No new token/refresh exposure; the single-owner auto-login posture is unchanged.

ensure_desktop_admin(pool): has_admin via UserRepository::new(pool), create via AppRepository::new(pool).create_admin_user(admin/admin@localhost/hash) + ziee::hash_password. Same repos Repos.{user,app} build from the same pool; owner identity + create semantics identical. Create stays app-side (BA decision).

pub use AppRepository + UserRepository are Rust type re-exports with NO JsonSchema/aide surface — they cannot enter the OpenAPI. E8 golden byte-identical (types.ts both surfaces) + canonical (openapi.json both surfaces) machine-confirms zero wire drift.

postgresql_embedded decl byte-matches the ziee server catalog (default-features=false + bundled,theseus,rustls); sqlx gains runtime-tokio-rustls+migrate and tokio gains rt-multi-thread+time — all already enabled by the server, so the unified src-app/Cargo.lock adds no new default features for the server binary. cargo check -p ziee + -p ziee-desktop exit 0.

SDK-standalone-only [env] POSTGRESQL_VERSION. Cargo config resolves from the invocation root, so when ziee consumes the SDK by path the ziee root's src-app/.cargo/config.toml wins graph-wide — this file is inert for ziee (cannot perturb its build). It only makes cd sdk && cargo … resolve ziee's exact PG version (fingerprint-identical → shared-target reuse). Verified: sdk workspace check exit 0.

Arg-only edits: ensure_desktop_admin(pool) / mint_admin_login(pool,&jwt) — the pool was already the in-scope shared_pool(). Zero assertion-body edits (E4). The 'Admin not found' contract assertion + jti-whitelist assertion are untouched.

grep -rn 'ziee::' over harness/src returns only two //! doc-comment prose lines; zero CODE references. Harness Cargo depends only on ziee-core/identity/auth, never the ziee app crate. skeleton-server (framework-only) still builds (E11). D-full is unblocked: ziee provides ZieeServerBoot; the harness consumes only ServerBoot + BootHandle.

boot() double-call guard (Mutex take() → 'called more than once'), jwt-not-captured guard, and start_server_with_routes error mapped to anyhow. The prior code had no double-call guard (start_backend_server was called once); the new guards are additive safety, never hit on the single-call live path.

SDK standalone lock grew ~921 lines (postgresql_embedded + postgresql_archive dep tree pulled into the SDK workspace). Expected + correct; no version conflicts (cd sdk && cargo check --workspace exit 0). ziee's src-app/Cargo.lock gained only the ziee-desktop→harness edge (+17).
